item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not get the end. Does that mean that the hallucination shows the view from that green orb from 2974? And that that orb is now at Foundation Site 94, Testing ground 002?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sad news. The millipede in the picture, Lester, died today. He just grew slower over the week and stopped moving. I left him die in peace over the night, and after seeing he's stone gone, I've buried him in a small grave in the forest. ;w;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally got around to reading the posted version. I'm very happy with the way you took the test log at the end and the way you cleaned up the containment procedures made it very readable.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy this. I think it breaks my usual distaste for huge implications, but you know. It's cute, and the imagery is cool, so. +1 from me.

EDIT: Oh, and some thangs I noticed. The capitalization in the footnotes is inconsistent (either start the footnotes with capitals or don't, you have both in different areas). "[…] constructing a line of Latin text using by producing abnormally patterned web." I think that "using" and "by producing" aren't supposed to both be in this sentence. I would get rid of using?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good imagery with the creatures. That being said, I am iffy about how the researcher induced a hallucination onto himself and that is counted as something worthy as evidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love the implications here, and I enjoyed it a lot as a sequel to 2794. Excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't a huge fan, myself, of 2794. This is pretty cool though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made as a sequel to SCP-2794. Thanks to everybody in the forums and chat who looked over this, and thank you to one of my pet Centrobolus Sp. (my male, nicknamed "Lester") for being a good boy and getting in the right position for the picture used.
